task.title: Associate durable tool invocations with active tasks
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/549 by immutably attributing durable tool invocation intent to an active task without changing legacy invocation histories.
acceptance_criteria: A task associated wrapper validates that the task exists and is active before authorization or adapter execution.
Intent commits only if the exact validated task stream version remains current, with no retry on a race.
The task ID survives load, list, reopen, and later task completion while version-one unassociated histories remain compatible.
Retention remains metadata-only and existing unassociated invocation behavior stays unchanged.

attempts.summary: Selected task level attribution before attempt correlation.
attempts.diagnostic: Provider tool activity can occur before an assistant attempt observation exists. Requiring attempt correlation would therefore impose ordering that the current runtime cannot guarantee.
attempts.patch: Decision issue 548 chose optional immutable TaskId attribution on invocation intent and deferred task side indexes, attempt relations, runtime orchestration, and richer retention.

attempts.summary: Established RED with task association integration tests.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused target failed because the task associated wrapper, task projection accessor, and typed missing, inactive, and changed-task errors did not exist.
attempts.patch: Retained tests for successful persistence, legacy replay, every terminal task state, missing tasks, duplicates, concurrent changes, reopen, list, and later task completion.

attempts.diagnostic: The store loads the active task and version, then atomically appends invocation intent only while that task stream is unchanged. Version-two intent carries TaskId; version-one intent replays with no association.
attempts.patch: Added invoke_tool_for_task_durable, optional task projection, additive event decoding, typed pre-execution errors, and shared terminal handling for associated and unassociated wrappers.

attempts.summary: Reviewed connection-count feedback without expanding scope.
attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit noted one extra SQLite connection as a trivial scalability consideration, not a correctness defect. Reworking store ownership would have changed a broader persistence architecture without evidence of a current problem.
attempts.patch: Recorded the connection observation as future maintainability evidence and retained the simple bounded composition. Independent review found no blocker and pinned verification passed.

attempts.diagnostic: Rust quality gate run 30053888051 succeeded. CodeRabbit passed the required status with only the non-blocking connection nitpick.
attempts.patch: Pull request 550 squash-merged as e007c0a2192919cc9fd31f87a3f6cc697f02172d and closed issue 549.

outcome.summary: Durable tool evidence can now be immutably attributed to an active task with race-safe intent persistence and backward-compatible replay.

verification.command: Independent pre-commit diff review # no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/actions/runs/30053888051 and CodeRabbit review at https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/pull/550

lessons: Task level attribution is useful before attempt-level correlation because tool activity may precede attempt evidence.
Validation plus conditional append closes the race between reading task status and committing invocation intent.
A task change should fail before authorization and execution rather than trigger an implicit retry.
Additive payload versions can preserve legacy unassociated histories while all new associated writes use one strict shape.
Non-blocking scalability notes should not force speculative store-ownership refactors inside a behavior slice.
